title: Mixed-mode AD: vector-parameter gradient (reverse tape) over inner derivative drops captured-parameter dependency — returns 0
goal: User-reported, verified. (gradient (lambda (p) (derivative (lambda (x) (* (vector-ref p 0) (* x x))) 2.0)) (vector 3.0)) returns #(0); must be #(4) since d/dx(theta x^2)@x=2 = 4 theta, d/dtheta = 4. Controls all correct: outer reverse-mode alone #(6); SCALAR gradient over derivative = 4 (the #84/#95 forward fast path); inner derivative alone = 12. Root cause (user's diagnosis, matches architecture): the outer VECTOR gradient uses the reverse tape; the inner derivative computes via forward jets and returns an ORDINARY number to the tape, so the dependency on tape-carrying captured values (vector-ref p 0) is dropped. derivative is not an AD-visible primitive to the reverse tape and has no backward rule for captured parameters.
fix_direction: Make the inner derivative propagate outer-AD dependencies through its captures. Two viable routes: (a) when an inner derivative runs while an outer reverse tape is active, detect captured operands that are tape nodes; seed each such capture with the SECOND jet slot (e2) and record the result on the outer tape with partial d(result)/d(capture) = the mixed e1e2 coefficient (the 4-jet already computes it — same machinery as #84 nested forward); (b) register derivative as a tape primitive whose backward runs the jet computation once per tape-carried capture. Route (a) mirrors the existing forward-over-forward nesting and the runtime __ad_pert_level discipline; prefer it. The scalar fast path (#95) must remain intact.
acceptance: multi-element: gradient over derivative with 2+ captured components each correct
scalar-mixed still 4; outer-only #(6); inner-only 12; nested_ad_loop_test + nested_ad_test + vm_ad all still pass
campaign_payoff: Completes the AD composition matrix: forward-over-forward (#84), scalar mixed (#95), now vector/reverse-over-forward. Directly requested by a downstream user.
